claim: Shifting methods share a common 5-phase structure: preparation, relaxation, trance induction, DR visualization, awakening attempt
Reality shifting originated circa 2019 on Spanish-language Tumblr and migrated to TikTok in 2020
The Raven Method requires lying in starfish position without moving for 30-60 minutes with counting 1-100
The Alice in Wonderland Method uses guided tunnel visualization and door-opening narrative
Neville Goddard's Law of Assumption is the most-cited non-community authority in shifting theory
The CIA Gateway Process document is frequently cited by shifters as government validation of consciousness transcendence
Shifting experiences are physiologically consistent with wake-initiated lucid dreams (WILD)
High absorption personality trait predicts shifting success
Shifting has been associated with depersonalization-derealization symptoms in vulnerable practitioners
The shifting community is predominantly female (70%), age 15-22
Approximately 40% of practitioners report having shifted at least once
Scripting is a core practice involving handwritten or digital DR descriptions
Binaural beats and subliminals are used by ~35% of shifters regularly
Scientific evidence for binaural beat efficacy is mixed with small effects on anxiety
Post-shift depression is a well-attested phenomenon where CR seems lacking after DR return
Clone theory (autonomous CR copy during shift) is controversial within the community
Respawning (permanent shift with no return) has raised concerns about suicidal ideation
No peer-reviewed clinical studies of reality shifting exist as of 2026
Academic literature on shifting is limited to 3+ published papers and several theses
Buddhist dream yoga is occasionally referenced as precedent for conscious awareness during sleep
The lay interpretation of quantum many-worlds is used to justify shifting metaphysics
Shifting methods converge on a protocol similar to hypnotic induction
Non-English shifting communities exist in Spanish, Portuguese, French, Russian, and Japanese
COVID-19 lockdowns likely accelerated shifting's spread due to adolescent confinement
Maladaptive daydreaming research has identified shifting as a variant
Hypnagogic state (wake-to-sleep transition) produces symptoms consistent with shifting symptoms
The most popular desired realities are Hogwarts (30%), Marvel/DC (15%), and anime worlds (15%)
The community rejects outsider skepticism with 'you have to experience it' framing
Robert Monroe's astral projection literature influenced energy-based shifting methods
Subliminal tracks in shifting use masked affirmations and claimed proprietary audio technology
Social contagion theory has been applied to shifting's spread pattern
The median number of attempts before first successful shift is 15-50
Shifting platform ecosystem includes TikTok, Reddit, Wattpad, YouTube, Tumblr, Discord, and Amino
Commercialization of shifting includes paid guides, coaching, and subliminal sales
Physicists and skeptics critique the misuse of quantum mechanics in shifting theory
Thomas Campbell's 'My Big TOE' is cited as a bridge between physics and conscious reality selection
No IRB-approved clinical studies of shifting practice have been published
The practice may be transitioning from fad to established practice with stable core practitioners
Shifting terminology includes CR, DR, mini-shift, permashift, respawn, scripting, symptoms, clone theory
The evidence base consists entirely of self-reported survey data and anecdotal reports
